description_long: This object is a classical-style bollard resembling a fluted Greco-Roman column, typically used as an access control barrier in urban environments. It is cylindrical with vertical grooves running along its length, giving it a decorative and robust appearance. The top is flat and slightly wider than the shaft, while the base is also flared, providing stability and a visually balanced profile. The surface appears to be made of a light-colored stone or concrete, with subtle weathering and minor imperfections visible throughout. There are no visible reflective markings, chains, or connectors, and the design is solid and monolithic. This bollard would be well-suited for placement on sidewalks, plazas, or at the entrances to pedestrian zones.
description: A fluted, column-like bollard with a flat top and flared base, made of light-colored stone or concrete. Its classical design and robust build make it ideal for urban access control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full fluted column design, with the flat top and flared base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical grooves and the uniform cylindrical shape, with minor surface imperfections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same fluted pattern and the slightly wider top and base, with subtle weathering.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the consistent fluted texture and solid construction.
